title: Bounded Promise Concurrency, Queueing, Ordering, Backpressure, and Cancellation · Inference Brief: Pokedex Ts Concurrency Queue Budget
summary: Promise.all launches one fetch per result, queues grow without limits, abort rejects the aggregate and hides partial evidence, completion order changes output, and background rejections escape. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts concurrency queue budget increment in bounded abortable detail-prefetch scheduler. Learners progr…
objectives: Set active, queued, byte, request, time, and retry budgets independent of result count.
Justify prefetch from learner latency and API fair-use evidence and identify views where it must remain disabled.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Concurrency Queue Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 8653 during an offline mobile-library catalog migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce async queue and concurrency budgets, ordering, partial results, abort, and rejection ownership; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Set active, queued, byte, request, time, and retry budgets independent of result count.
